title: Critical Perspectives on the Bio-Economy

prerequisites.text: Acceptance for Honours or Master's specialising in EGS or cognate disciplines.

outline: Located at the interface of fast-changing genetic and information technologies and a range of social, environmental and ethical concerns, the bio-economy has changed ways in which biodiversity is used, conserved and commercialised. Using a political ecology framing, this interdisciplinary course introduces key theories that situate the bio-economy and deepens understanding of emerging debates — from contestations about genetically modified crops and 'biopiracy' charges, to the potential of agroecology as a sustainable agricultural future. The course draws on research mostly from Sub-Saharan Africa and includes several short fieldtrips. Students are expected to read set texts, watch set videos and prepare seminars.
dp_requirements: At least 80% attendance and submission of all assignments.
assessment: 1 exam – 40%; 1 essay (3000–4000 words) – 30%; 1 seminar presentation – 15%; 1 critique (response to a particular article, policy or media piece) – 15%.
